intro: The clearest split between Weblate and react-intl is how they charge: Weblate uses a free tier plus paid plans, while react-intl uses free and open source. Weblate specializes in Translation Management; it fits best for Global products launching in multiple markets. react-intl specializes in Internationalization; it fits best for Global products launching in multiple markets. Pick Weblate for Global products launching in multiple markets; pick react-intl for Global products launching in multiple markets. Weblate is not for single-language apps or internal tools. react-intl is not for single-language apps or internal tools. Honest trade-offs: Weblate is weak for machine translation quality varies by language pair, and react-intl is weak for machine translation quality varies by language pair — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Localization / Translation API umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Weblate is for global products launching in multiple markets; react-intl is for global products launching in multiple markets; decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Weblate if you want a free tier plus paid plans and your workload matches global products launching in multiple markets.
verdict_b: Choose react-intl if you want free and open source and your workload matches global products launching in multiple markets.

faq.a: react-intl is the cheaper option on paper since it is open source, while Weblate is paid. Weblate offers a free tier. react-intl is free and open source.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Weblate to react-intl?
faq.a: Migration between these is relatively straightforward since both are rated as low lock-in. Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

react-intl scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for Weblate). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is react-intl a good alternative to Weblate?
faq.a: react-intl sits in the same category as Weblate, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. react-intl is best fit for global products launching in multiple markets. Note: react-intl is not for single-language apps or internal tools.
